title: About Me
text:
ℹ️ About Me
    📊 Hot 100 Chart
    🎧 Mood Snapshot
  









Hi, I’m Mario

Welcome to my project!
This dashboard is part of a personal learning end-to-end analytics project. It pulls from multiple APIs and walks through ingesting, cleaning, and analyzing real-time data.
The first page of this dashboard was inspired by Melissa Van Bussel’s 2024 Posit Table Contest submission, which served as a template and educational reference.
The current version includes a lightweight data pipeline that refreshes Spotify Hot 100 track data, manages cache files for weekly updates, and enriches chart tracks with audio features for analysis-ready outputs. The mood page extends the project with audio-feature analysis and supporting visualizations.
If you’d like to connect, you can find me here:


GitHub: github.com/MariojReyes
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/marioreyesiii

1 The popularity of a track is a value between 0 and 100, with 100 being the most popular. The popularity is calculated by algorithm and is based, in the most part, on the total number of plays the track has had and how recent those plays are. Generally speaking, songs that are being played a lot now will have a higher popularity than songs that were played a lot in the past.


Source: Billboard Hot 100 Playlist, from the Spotify Web API

title: Mood of the Week — Billboard Hot 100
text:
ℹ️ About Me
  📊 Hot 100 Chart
  🎧 Mood Snapshot


Mood Index is a composite score from valence, energy, danceability, and tempo (scaled 0–100), shown as both track-average and popularity-weighted exposure.


KPIs


       Mood Index
       56.1
       55.9 weighted
     
       Avg Valence
       0.471
       0.469 weighted
     
       Avg Energy
       0.628
       0.624 weighted
     
       Median Tempo
       125 BPM
        
     

Energy × Valence











Distributions











Archetype Composition


Popularity-weighted share is computed as each archetype’s sum of Spotify popularity divided by the total popularity sum across all Hot 100 tracks this week.

Song Preview


hot100 = await FileAttachment("data/hot100_ojs.json").json()

viewof songDropdown = Inputs.select(
  hot100,
  {
    label: "Choose a song",
    format: d => `${d.song_name} — ${d.artist_name}`,
    unique: true
  }
)
















html`<iframe
  style="border-radius:12px;margin-top:10px;display:block;"
  src=${`https://open.spotify.com/embed/track/${songDropdown.song_id}?utm_source=generator&theme=0`}
  width="100%"
  height="180"
  frameBorder="0"
  allowfullscreen=""
  allow="autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; fullscreen; picture-in-picture"
  loading="lazy">
</iframe>`







Mood is inferred from Spotify audio features — an aggregate signal of track acoustics, not direct listener emotion. Popularity weighting proxies exposure. Tracking started March 15, 2026.
